sourceRef.citation: Yaswanthkumar S. and Venkitachalam R. et al. 2026

statement.en: 'ECTICON' - A Smart Android Application for the Identi ication and Management of Ectoparasitic Flies of Cattle
statement.summary: Parasitic lies belong to the order Diptera which possess evolved mouthparts adapted for blood feeding and acts as mechanical and biological vectors for pathogens (Desquesnes et al., 2018). Anong the lies, Musca, Stomoxys, Haematobia, Tabanus and Hippobosca were the major lies cause irritation, blood loss, stress and reduced productivity in cattle (Strydom et al., 2025). More importantly, many of these lies act as vectors for various disease-causing pathogens and posing serious threats to animal health and farm economics (Byford et al., 1992). Despite their impact, accurate identi ication of ectoparasitic lies remains dif icult at the ield level. Farmers and livestock workers often rely on guesswork or delayed expert consultation, which can lead to improper control practices (Smythe, 2018).

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper describes a software application (ECTICON) currently under development/validation as of 2026-04-25. The artifact itself exists as a provisional tool, not yet peer-validated in published form. The underlying biological claims (parasitic fly identification, vector competence) are CLASS-B established, but the AI application's efficacy, accuracy rates, and field deployment readiness remain unverified—characteristic of FLOWING status pending validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 農家の判断速度がシグモイド曲線に沿って牛の健康を決める — 同定が遅れるほど、生産性は指数関数的に崩壊する。

visualGlyph.interpretation: ハエの目が光り、農家の判断を求める — 中央を貫く時間軸が、診断の遅延というリスクを象徴する。
